---
title: "Examples"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Examples}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>", fig.align = "center")
```

This example uses European anchovy (*Engraulis encrasicolus*) in the
Mediterranean Sea. The analysis asks how projected marine climate conditions
for areas with current SDM support are displaced relative to the species'
current realised climatic niche.

The vignette uses small output tables and prebuilt figures so it can run quickly
on CRAN. The full workflow used the same `climniche` functions shown below.

```{r}
library(climniche)

case_path <- system.file("extdata/mediterranean_anchovy", package = "climniche")
if (identical(case_path, "")) {
  case_path <- file.path("..", "inst", "extdata", "mediterranean_anchovy")
  if (!dir.exists(case_path)) {
    case_path <- file.path("inst", "extdata", "mediterranean_anchovy")
  }
}
summary_tab <- read.csv(file.path(case_path, "anchovy_climniche_summary.csv"))
class_tab <- read.csv(file.path(case_path, "anchovy_climniche_classes.csv"))
variable_tab <- read.csv(file.path(case_path, "anchovy_climniche_top_variables.csv"))
screen_tab <- read.csv(file.path(case_path, "anchovy_predictor_screen.csv"))
settings_tab <- read.csv(file.path(case_path, "anchovy_climniche_settings.csv"))
records <- read.csv(file.path(case_path, "anchovy_clean_obis_records.csv"))
```

## Case design

Occurrence records were downloaded from OBIS and clipped to the Mediterranean
Sea. Environmental layers were downloaded from Bio-ORACLE v3 using the
`biooracler` interface. The current layers describe baseline surface ocean
conditions; the future layers describe the same variables under the selected
future scenario and year.

```{r}
settings_tab[settings_tab$setting %in% c(
  "species",
  "region",
  "environmental_data_source",
  "future_scenario",
  "future_year"
), ]

nrow(records)
```

The SDM was fitted from occurrence and background cells. Its suitability
threshold was selected by maximising test-set TSS, rather than by imposing a
manual suitability cut-off. The continuous suitability map was then used as
reference weights in `fit_climniche_terra()`. The threshold only removes low
suitability cells; values above the threshold remain continuous weights.

```{r}
settings_tab[settings_tab$setting %in% c(
  "sdm_threshold_method",
  "sdm_threshold",
  "sdm_test_tss",
  "sdm_test_sensitivity",
  "sdm_test_specificity"
), ]
```

Nine Bio-ORACLE variables were considered before fitting. Pairwise correlation
and VIF screening retained a multivariate climate set for both the SDM and the
climniche exposure calculation.

```{r}
settings_tab[settings_tab$setting %in% c(
  "predictor_correlation_cutoff",
  "predictor_vif_limit",
  "minimum_retained_predictors",
  "sdm_retained_predictor_count",
  "exposure_retained_predictor_count"
), ]

screen_tab[screen_tab$retained, c(
  "role", "label", "vif_after_screening"
)]
```

The fitted object stores the effective classification settings used for this
case. These values can be supplied directly by the user or estimated from the
fitted metrics through the quantile arguments.

```{r}
settings_tab[settings_tab$setting %in% c(
  "boundary_quantile",
  "tolerance",
  "stable_climate_change",
  "stable_reconfiguration",
  "boundary_exceedance_tolerance",
  "conflict_ratio"
), ]
```

## Outputs

The summary table reports the main quantities over current reference cells.
Niche Distance Shift is signed, so positive and negative values distinguish
movement away from and toward the current realised niche centre. Niche Boundary
Exceedance is a magnitude beyond the empirical boundary, not only an inside or
outside flag.

```{r}
summary_tab[, c(
  "n", "reference_weight_sum", "boundary_quantile",
  "mean_climate_change_amount", "mean_niche_distance_change",
  "prop_niche_divergence", "prop_niche_convergence",
  "prop_niche_exceedance", "prop_climate_reconfiguration"
)]

class_tab
```

Variable contributions summarise which predictors contribute most to the fitted
change metric. They should be read as model-derived contributions, not as
causal attribution.

```{r}
variable_tab
```

## Mediterranean maps

The first panel shows the continuous current reference weights after applying
the SDM threshold. The second panel shows the derived exposure classes. The
remaining panels show the four continuous metrics used by `climniche`.

```{r, echo = FALSE, out.width = "100%"}
knitr::include_graphics("figures/anchovy-mediterranean-maps.png")
```

Exposure classes are derived from the continuous metrics after fitting. They
are not one class per metric.

## Summary figure

`plot_climniche_showcase()` combines the binned exposure plane, exposure class
proportions, variable contributions and metric distributions.

```{r, echo = FALSE, out.width = "95%"}
knitr::include_graphics("figures/anchovy-climniche-showcase.png")
```

The binned plane separates Climatic Displacement from Niche Distance Shift.
Cells with similar projected climate displacement can differ in whether future
conditions move toward or away from the realised niche centre, and only a
subset exceeds the empirical niche boundary.

## Reproducing the workflow

The raster workflow needs aligned current and future environmental layers.
`occupied` can be a binary SDM map or a continuous SDM suitability map. With
a continuous suitability map, `occupied_threshold` removes low suitability
cells but does not convert the remaining values to 1.

```r
sdm_threshold <- as.numeric(
  settings_tab$value[settings_tab$setting == "sdm_threshold"]
)
selected_predictors <- screen_tab$variable[
  screen_tab$role == "climniche exposure calculation" & screen_tab$retained
]

fit <- fit_climniche_terra(
  current = current_layers[[selected_predictors]],
  future = future_layers[[selected_predictors]],
  occupied = anchovy_sdm_suitability,
  occupied_threshold = sdm_threshold,
  domain = mediterranean_domain,
  domain_threshold = sdm_threshold,
  sensitivity = predictor_sensitivity,
  boundary = 0.95
)

climniche_summary(fit)
plot_climniche_map(fit, metric = "niche_distance_change",
                   occupied = anchovy_sdm_suitability,
                   occupied_only = TRUE,
                   occupied_threshold = sdm_threshold)
plot_climniche_classes(fit, occupied = anchovy_sdm_suitability,
                       occupied_only = TRUE,
                       occupied_threshold = sdm_threshold)
plot_climniche_showcase(fit)
```

The same calculation can be run from matrices after raster values have been
extracted.

```r
fit <- fit_climniche(
  current = current_values[, selected_predictors],
  future = future_values[, selected_predictors],
  occupied = sdm_suitability,
  occupied_threshold = sdm_threshold,
  sensitivity = predictor_sensitivity,
  boundary = 0.95
)
```
